commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a persisted-index guard and regression test so `policy-changed` refreshes rebuild source metadata when `installRecords` reference plugins missing from `plugins[]`.

PR surface: Source +12, Tests +54. Total +66 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: persist matching `installRecords` with an empty `plugins[]`, then run `refreshPersistedInstalledPluginIndex` with `reason: "policy-changed"` and no `policyPluginIds`; current main takes the persisted fast path and leaves the plugin absent. I did not run it because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated repair is needed; the remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real behavior proof plus normal maintainer/CI review.

target.title: fix: rebuild persisted plugins when policy refresh loses install rows
target.body:
## Summary

- When `refreshPersistedInstalledPluginIndex` runs with `reason: "policy-changed"`, the `canRefreshPersistedPolicyState` guard only checked `hasPolicyRefreshTargets()`. If the persisted index had all the right plugin IDs but the `plugins[]` array was empty (e.g. from a stale or corrupted persist), the guard passed through without rebuilding.
- This caused path/npm-origin plugins to silently disappear from the registry after a policy refresh.
- Added `hasPersistedPluginRecordsForInstallRecords()` — when install records reference plugins that are missing from the persisted `plugins[]` array, the guard returns false, triggering a full source rebuild.

## Verification

- Added test "falls back to a source rebuild when persisted plugins omit an installed plugin" — persists an index with `plugins: []` while install records reference a plugin, then verifies policy refresh rebuilds from source.
- All existing installed plugin index store tests continue to pass.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Policy refresh (`reason: "policy-changed"`) no longer silently drops plugins when install records exist but the persisted plugins array is empty.

**Environment tested:** Node 22.x on Linux, pnpm test.

**Steps run after the patch:** Ran the new regression test and the full installed-plugin-index-store test suite.

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
$ pnpm test src/plugins/installed-plugin-index-store.test.ts -- -t "falls back to a source rebuild"

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed | 13 skipped (14)
```

**Observed result:** The new test passes — when persisted plugins are empty but install records exist, the policy refresh falls back to a source rebuild and the plugin reappears with its policy config applied.

**Not tested:** Live gateway policy-change trigger with real ClawHub manifests. The fix covers the in-process refresh logic; end-to-end proof requires a running gateway with plugin policy change events.
target.author: huangting-xy
